author: y4rr
content:
Pass 5.A.4 surfaced 'closed-tracker drift' as a new accreted failure mode for the series:

- Skeleton modules (gloo/mpi/ucc) cited closed #459 in source/test docstrings + Cargo.toml comments
- gpu_collective NCCL placeholder cited closed #668 in 7 source sites + 6 #[ignore] attributes
- All 4 fix-phase audit-cited STUBs (Pass 5.A.4 #1102) were stale-no-op (correct fail-fast contract); the real bug was untracked deferred work, not silent corruption.

Pattern prediction: any audit finding citing 'STUB' in a feature-gated module is suspect; verify (a) is the feature-off path silent or fail-fast, (b) does the deferred-work tracker exist and stay open. If (a) is fail-fast and (b) is closed/missing, it's closed-tracker drift, not a STUB.

Tally: Pass 5 has now caught 1 missing test, 5 stale-MPS findings, 1 cfg-gate-vs-Linux-runnable architect oversight, 2 architect-side pre-flight issues (ZERO-match floor + missed file). Audit signal-to-noise on STUB findings is approximately 30-40% real bugs (Pass 5.A.1 SSD300 was real; 5.A.2 was 3/8 real; 5.A.3 was 1/1 real; 5.A.4 was 0/3 real but surfaced closed-tracker drift).
